summary: Terminal Ramsey/T2star scout completed with saved data, healthy counts, no stop request, and no drift flags. Terminal analysis supports a weak empirical Ramsey oscillation near 1.9 MHz and short/few-us T2* order, but not a final scalar T2star; 13C is not established.

Terminal 8-average Ramsey/T2star scout completed and was raw-exported, plotted, drift-checked, FFT-reviewed, and fit-tested. Counts were healthy (pre-run TrackCenter 43.710 kcps, post-run final 41.020 kcps), scan matched 43 tau points over 0-8 us with det=1.5 MHz and 8 x 50000 reps, and scan-order-aware drift found no flagged averages. Signal readout supports a weak empirical Ramsey oscillation near 1.9 MHz; fit-derived T2* is short/few-us but not a final scalar because early-time handling changes the result. The nominal det carrier is weak and the apparent high-sideband power is ambiguous with an effective carrier shift, so 13C is not established. Recommended next step is bounded weak-pi pODMR resonance refinement before a claim-grade repeat Ramsey/13C test.

summary: Provisional 3-average autosave review of the running weak-pi pODMR refinement. The bridge job remains running at average index 4 with healthy counts and no stop request. recent_evidence.summary: Terminal 8-average Ramsey/T2star scout completed and was raw-exported, plotted, drift-checked, FFT-reviewed, and fit-tested. Counts were healthy (pre-run TrackCenter 43.710 kcps, post-run final 41.020 kcps), scan matched 43 tau points over 0-8 us with det=1.5 MHz and 8 x 50000 reps, and scan-order-aware drift found no flagged averages. Signal readout supports a weak empirical Ramsey oscillation near 1.9 MHz; fit-derived T2* is short/few-us but not a final scalar because early-time handling changes the result. The nominal det carrier is weak and the apparent high-sideband power is ambiguous with an effective carrier shift, so 13C is not established. Recommended next step is bounded weak-pi pODMR resonance refinement before a claim-grade repeat Ramsey/13C test.

recent_evidence.summary: Bridge-free weak-signal follow-up condition review after terminal det=1.25 Ramsey. The physical signature is a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate, but Ramsey remains ambiguous because target amplitudes are only about 0.5-1.0% and low-sideband/direct-Larmor/carrier support is incomplete. A non-blind follow-up candidate is auto__cpmg / CPMG.xml with N=8 and tau 0.45..1.60 us to cover both 1/(4 f13)=0.650 us and 1/(2 f13)=1.300 us conventions for f13=384.6 kHz. No bridge job was submitted; next bridge-touching work needs fresh intent, verifier, MATLAB advisory, queue-idle check, and current drift/window review.

c02 terminal det=1.25 Ramsey review and weak-13C follow-up condition review: Terminal det=1.25 MHz Ramsey for reimage1804_c02 completed cleanly at 06:41 EDT with healthy counts (44.073 kcps pre-run, 42.669 kcps post-run), 16 x 50000 reps over tau 0..10 us, snake scan order, and no drift flags. Readout2/self-baseline FFT target bins were direct 13C 0.54%, det-13C 0.79%, carrier 1.00%, det+13C 1.01%, old det=1 high/static 0.39%, static low 0.46%, previous 1.9 MHz 0.23%. Cross-det comparison keeps a weak det-shift-consistent high-sideband candidate (1.95/0.84/1.01% for det=1.5/1.0/1.25), but it is not a claim-grade Ramsey-only 13C observation. T2star is supported as short/few-us order, not a precise scalar. A bridge-free follow-up review identified a bounded CPMG tau-scan candidate but did not submit bridge work.
